name: Sur Le Gril Chopped Liver
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the tough outer membranes from the liver using a sharp knife.
2. In a heavy skillet, heat oil and add chopped onions. Cook over medium-high heat until translucent and golden brown. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.
3. Add butter to the skillet and melt it. Add liver pieces. Fry liver over medium heat for 3 minutes on each side, or brush with butter and broil if preferred. Test for doneness by cutting a piece; if still pink, continue cooking for 30-60 seconds.
4. Force the egg through a wire strainer to create a finely mashed egg mixture that disappears in the liver mixture. Sieve the separated hard-cooked whites and yolks separately.
5. Fit a meat grinder with a medium blade and grind the cooked liver and onions. Reground the ground mixture.
6. Combine the twice-ground meat and onion mixture with the sieved eggs. Add salt, pepper, and optionally a few drops of commercial gravy browning liquid to enhance color. Taste and adjust seasoning.
7. Refrigerate for 24 hours before serving. Serve with slices of tomato, cucumber, and dark rye bread.
Serves 4.
